channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ozempic and similar weight loss drugs may lower risk of 42 health conditions, but also pose risks
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration’s approval of Ozempic in 2021 for weight loss treatment ushered in a new era for the class of drugs called glucagon-like peptide-1 agonists, or GLP-1.
item.dc:creator: Ziyad Al-Aly Clinical Epidemiologist, Washington University in St. Louis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 tips for launching a side hustle at any age
item.description: Either start your own business or join an existing side-hustle platform that matches people with opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, WGN 9

item.title: Your wages will stretch the furthest in these major US metros, study finds
item.description: A new study by finance site Bankrate found that, after adjusting for the cost of living, the average worker's buying power only grew (compared to the other cities) in a handful of large metros.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former NBA star marks two years of his cannabis shop in St. Louis
item.description: There was celebration and appreciation in downtown St. Louis Friday as former NBA star Al Harrington marked the two-year anniversary of Viola Cannabis Dispensary.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Harris, Fox 5

item.title: TSA officer reportedly caught with firearm in busy Atlanta airport is arrested
item.description: Police arrest a TSA officer who was allegedly carrying a firearm beyond the security checkpoint at Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport, a major travel hub.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As schools in LA reopen, parents worry about harmful ash from wildfires
item.description: Weeks after the worst of the deadly fires tore through Los Angeles suburbs, families are grappling with the reality that toxic ash from burned cars and homes could be a health issue for awhile, especially for children.
item.dc:creator: Dorany Pineda And Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ICE agents raid New Jersey seafood store, detain US military veteran
item.description: The store owner told NewsNation affiliate WPIX that a U.S. military veteran was among the people detained.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about the ruling blocking Trump's order on birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump's executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally has faced the first of what will be many legal tests. It didn't fare well.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Asylum-seekers pushed to new extremes in Mexico after Trump's border crackdown begins
item.description: ATOTONILCO DE TULA, Mexico (AP) — When Dayana Castro heard that the U.S. asylum appointment she waited over a year for was canceled in an instant, she had no doubt: She was heading north any way she could.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY and EDGAR H. CLEMENTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pro-life protesters pardoned by Trump, Fox confirms
item.description: President Donald Trump issues pardons for pro-life activists convicted under the FACE act during former President Joe Biden's administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mississippi state lawmaker introduces 'Contraception Begins at Erection Act' with fines for masturbation
item.description: A Democratic Mississippi state senator introduced legislation this week that makes it unlawful for men to masturbate "without the intent to fertilize an embryo," with the lawmaker criticizing anti-abortion measures that only "focus on the woman’s role.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Just days into the new Trump administration, worries spike amid a show of force on immigration
item.description: Less than a week into his presidency, the Trump administration is touting deportation efforts and has published new rules making it easier to remove people.
item.dc:creator: Mike Catalini And Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: 2025 Oscar nominations: The biggest snubs and surprises
item.description: The members of the film academy voted to honor a few outliers — and overlook a couple of hopeful contenders — at the 97th Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, ABC 4

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: A Florida police officer is without a job after accidentally discharging a man's gun during a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Convicted killer sings in front of victims' families
item.description: A man convicted of murdering two women in 1986 interrupted his own sentencing hearing on Tuesday by singing in front of his victims' families.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WGN 9

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally, calling it “blatantly unconstitutional” during the first hearing in a multi-state effort challenging the order.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Schumer calls on Trump to declassify government files on UFOs
item.description: “Now do UFOs,” Schumer wrote on social platform X in a repost of the White House’s announcement that Trump has ordered government files on John F.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: The officer "had difficulty removing" the gun, and while trying to do so, it fired, striking the driver in the leg, the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Gulf of Whatnow? Trump's geographic renaming plans leave mapmakers pondering what to do next
item.description: What's in a name change, after all? The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man who laundered money for Chicago-based drug trafficking organization handed 8-year sentence after 15 years on the run
item.description: CHICAGO — A man who laundered money for a Chicago-based drug trafficking organization was handed an eight-year federal prison sentence this week after spending more than a decade on the run.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about President Donald Trump's order targeting the rights of transgender people
item.description: An executive order President Donald Trump signed on his first day back in office offers a new federal government definition of the sexes that could have a major impact on transgender people nationwide.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Purdue Pharma and owners to pay $7.4 billion in settlement of lawsuits over the toll of OxyContin
item.description: Members of the family who own OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma, and the company itself, agreed to pay up to $7.4 billion in a new settlement to lawsuits over the toll of the powerful prescription painkiller, the attorneys general from several states announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CNN is announcing layoffs as part of a further shift to digital business
item.description: CNN announced a restructuring Thursday that includes some 200 layoffs, an accelerated pivot to digital operations and new TV roles for personalities like Wolf Blitzer, Jake Tapper and Audie Cornish.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ICE arrests nearly 500 in nationwide immigration crackdown that includes Illinois
item.description: Federal immigration agents conducted the first large-scale arrest operation under the Trump administration, resulting in nearly 500 arrests.
item.dc:creator: John Clark, WGN 9

item.title: Former Arkansas officer arrested, charged with assault in beating of handcuffed inmate
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A former Arkansas police officer who was caught on video beating a handcuffed inmate in the back of his patrol car last year has been arrested and charged with aggravated assault.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta denies platforms are forcing users to follow Trump, Vance
item.description: Meta is pushing back on claims that Facebook and Instagram users are being forced into following accounts for President Trump and Vice President Vance.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Fire chief shot, killed after stopping to help driver who struck a deer in Alabama
item.description: “We lost one of our brothers, leaders, mentor, and family to a senseless tragedy,” Cauthen's colleagues wrote in a Facebook post.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth White, Fox 5

item.title: Man who got Jan. 6 pardon is arrested on federal gun charge
item.description: The Florida man appears to be the first Jan. 6 rioter to face additional legal trouble after the sweeping clemency bestowed on those who rioted at the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Teen fatally shoots 16-year-old girl, himself at Tennessee high school: police
item.description: A student is dead and another injured after a teen gunman opened fire at a Nashville area high school before turning the gun on himself, police say.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, ABC 4

item.title: Mexico setting up tents for migrants deported by Trump
item.description: Mexico on Tuesday continued to prepare for President Donald Trump’s promised mass deportations by setting up the first of nine migrant attention centers on the border.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to investigate local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, ABC 4

item.title: 5 takeaways on Trump's executive actions on gender
item.description: President Donald Trump signed a sweeping executive order late Monday during his first hours in office recognizing only two sexes and preventing government dollars from being spent on what his administration called “gender ideology.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9
